hero.headline: Shoolini Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Pay After Placement and NAAC A+ accreditation

sections.tldr: Shoolini Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers talent acquisition, learning and development, performance management, industrial relations, HR analytics, and compensation design. PAP option Rs 1,50,000 or opt-out Rs 1,30,000. LinkedIn Learning access. KPMG certifications. NAAC A+. NIRF #69 University. India's only online MBA with Pay After Placement.

about.body:
Human Resource Management is a consistent high-employment MBA specialisation, with growing demand across IT services, BFSI, manufacturing, healthcare, and retail. HR Managers and L&D specialists are required at every organisation with 50 or more employees, making HRM one of the most stable career paths in management.

Shoolini University Online's MBA in HRM builds capabilities across talent acquisition and workforce planning, learning and development, performance management systems, industrial relations and labour law, compensation and benefits design, and HR analytics. The programme is designed for working professionals in HR executive roles transitioning into manager positions, and for non-HR professionals seeking to move into people management.

Shoolini University holds NIRF #69 (University, 2025) and NAAC A+ accreditation. The Pay After Placement option on Track 1 allows candidates to defer Rs 30,000 of the Rs 1,50,000 effective fee until after confirmed placement - a feature no other UGC-DEB approved online MBA offers in India.

Bundled benefits include LinkedIn Learning access, KPMG certifications, Adda247 government exam preparation, and an AI interview preparation tool. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are common core for all Shoolini MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 specialisation-specific subjects are provided by Shoolini at the admission stage. Contact our counsellor for the full HRM Sem 3-4 curriculum.

subjects.name: Marketing Management
subjects.desc: Core marketing principles, market segmentation, consumer decision models, and B2B and B2C strategy fundamentals.

subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
subjects.desc: Team dynamics, motivation theories, leadership styles, conflict resolution, and organisational culture frameworks.

subjects.name: Financial Accounting
subjects.desc: Preparation of financial statements, accounting standards, ratio analysis, and management reporting basics.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
subjects.desc: Business model design, lean startup methodology, venture capital basics, and opportunity identification frameworks.

subjects.name: Creativity Decoded
subjects.desc: Creative problem-solving models, SCAMPER technique, lateral thinking, and innovation culture building.

subjects.name: Digital and Technological Solutions
subjects.desc: Digital transformation frameworks, cloud basics, ERP overview, and technology adoption in business management.

subjects.name: Functional English
subjects.desc: Business writing, email communication, report preparation, and professional oral communication skills.

subjects.name: Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: Recruitment and selection, training and development, performance management, and compensation design.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.desc: Capital structure, working capital management, capital budgeting, and cost of capital analysis.

subjects.name: Career and Life Skills
subjects.desc: Goal setting, personal effectiveness, professional networking, and career planning frameworks.

subjects.name: Managerial Economics
subjects.desc: Demand analysis, pricing strategies, market structures, and business forecasting methods.

subjects.name: Statistics for Management
subjects.desc: Descriptive statistics, probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and regression for business.

subjects.name: Functional English - 2
subjects.desc: Advanced business writing, executive summary preparation, and formal professional correspondence.

subjects.name: Presentation and Charts
subjects.desc: Data visualisation tools, chart selection, slide design principles, and storytelling with data.

subjects.name: Fundamentals of Direct Selling
subjects.desc: Direct selling business models, DSA regulatory framework, ethics in direct sales, and network building.

whoHires.body:
HRM MBA graduates from Shoolini University Online are hired across industries requiring structured people management:

**Industries:** IT services, BFSI, manufacturing, FMCG, healthcare, retail, logistics, hospitality, and government PSUs

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, L&D Coordinator, HR Generalist, Payroll Executive, Employee Engagement Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, HRBP (HR Business Partner), Compensation and Benefits Manager, Industrial Relations Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, VP Human Resources, Chief People Officer, Head of Talent Management, Head of L&D, Global HRBP

**Top hiring organisations:** Infosys, TCS, Wipro, HCL, Accenture, Deloitte, KPMG, SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Bajaj Auto, Hero MotoCorp, Maruti Suzuki, Apollo Hospitals, Fortis Healthcare, and mid-market companies across Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand manufacturing corridors

skills.body:
The HRM specialisation builds skills that IT, manufacturing, and BFSI employers test at HR executive and manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Talent Acquisition: job description writing, structured interview frameworks, competency-based assessment design, and ATS navigation
- Performance Management: KRA and KPI setting, OKR frameworks, 360-degree feedback process design, and performance improvement plan writing
- Compensation Design: CTC structuring, variable pay design, benefits benchmarking, and salary band construction
- Labour Law: Industrial Disputes Act, Shops and Establishments Act, PF and ESIC compliance, and contract labour management

**Applied skills:**
- HR Analytics: attrition modelling, workforce planning spreadsheets, HR dashboard design, and HRIS data interpretation
- L&D: training need analysis, learning pathway design, ROI of training measurement, and e-learning platform management

**Soft skills:** Mediating grievance resolution between employees and management, conducting sensitive performance conversations, presenting headcount and attrition data to senior leadership, and building trust-based relationships across organisational hierarchies.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Shoolini Online MBA HRM against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA Human Resource Management:** Amity has strong HRM brand with dedicated HR analytics content at NIRF #22. Fee is Rs 2,25,000. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 (opt-out) is 42% cheaper with NAAC A+. PAP track (Rs 1,50,000) is still 33% cheaper. For candidates valuing brand recognition in large IT or consulting firms, Amity's HR analytics specialisation is differentiated. For cost-conscious professionals or those wanting PAP security, Shoolini delivers equivalent NAAC A+ credentials at significantly lower cost.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Human Resource Management:** NMIMS HRM carries strong Mumbai corporate and financial sector recognition. Fee is approximately Rs 1,70,000-1,80,000. NMIMS does not offer PAP. Shoolini is 24-28% cheaper with NAAC A+ and NIRF #69. For North India manufacturing and IT sector HRM roles, Shoolini's fee advantage and PAP differentiator outweigh NMIMS brand premium.

**vs LPU Online MBA Human Resource Management:** LPU has NAAC A++ and a large campus network supporting placement connections. Fee is approximately Rs 1,50,000-1,80,000. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is comparably priced or cheaper. The PAP option is Shoolini's primary differentiator in this head-to-head.

fees.body:
The Shoolini University Online MBA in Human Resource Management carries a regular fee of Rs 2,00,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). A Rs 500 one-time application fee applies. No registration fee, no exam fees, no project fees beyond the programme fee.

**Two offer tracks**:

**Offer 1 - MBA with Pay After Placement (PAP)**:
- Rs 50,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,50,000 (25% savings).
- Payment split: Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 x 4 semesters) + Rs 30,000 payable after confirmed placement.

**Offer 2 - MBA Opt-out from PAP**:
- Rs 70,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,30,000 (35% savings).
- Triggered by the 20% opt-out PAP scholarship.
- Payment: Rs 32,500 x 4 semesters.

**Merit scholarship (10% additional)**:
For candidates scoring 90% or above in Class 12 (UG entry) or 90% or above in UG degree (PG entry). Non-combinable with PAP or opt-out tracks, stackable with lump-sum and annual discounts.

**Additional payment discounts (stackable with merit only)**:
- 10% off on full course fee with single lump-sum payment
- Additional 5% off on annual payment in full

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Shoolini University (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Shoolini each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Shoolini University MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards. NIRF #69 University and NAAC A+ accreditation are recognised by IT companies, BFSI employers, and manufacturing sector HR departments across India.

DigiLocker verification is available. The degree satisfies management degree requirements for HR Manager promotions at PSUs, IT companies, and banks that specify a recognised UGC-DEB entitled university qualification. LinkedIn Learning and KPMG certifications add measurable professional development credentials alongside the core degree.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Shoolini University Online MBA HRM alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Kavita S.
items.liked: PAP structure was ideal for a career-transition candidate - paid post-placement as promised
items.disliked: Industrial relations content could go deeper on specific state-level labour laws for Himachal Pradesh
items.body: I was transitioning from an admin role to HR at a manufacturing company in Himachal Pradesh. The PAP structure removed the financial risk from my career change - I paid Rs 30,000 post-placement only after securing my HR Executive role. Performance management and talent acquisition subjects were directly applicable. LinkedIn Learning HR courses supplemented the programme content well. NAAC A+ from Shoolini satisfied my new employer's degree requirement for the HR designation.

items.name: Anand P.
items.liked: Compensation design and HR analytics subjects were genuinely practical for my HRBP work
items.disliked: Some faculty live sessions had audio quality issues on the LMS platform
items.body: I work as an HRBP at an IT services company and enrolled to formalise my HR credential. Compensation benchmarking, OKR framework, and 360-degree feedback process design subjects matched exactly what I do at work. KPMG certification improved my profile for internal promotion discussions. NAAC A+ from NIRF #69 Shoolini satisfied my company's MBA eligibility requirement for the senior HRBP role. Zero-cost EMI made the opt-out track at Rs 1,30,000 very accessible.

items.name: Meera K.
items.liked: Labour law content covered PF, ESIC, and contract labour compliance practically
items.disliked: Placement support varied - more relevant for IT sector than manufacturing, which is my industry
items.body: I manage HR compliance for a manufacturing unit in Ludhiana and needed the management degree for my company's internal promotion criteria. Labour law coverage - PF and ESIC compliance, Industrial Disputes Act, Shops Act - was directly applicable. Statistics for Management in Sem 2 helped me build basic attrition tracking models. The Rs 1,30,000 opt-out fee at NAAC A+ was the right price-quality combination for my situation.

faqs.question: What is Shoolini's Pay After Placement option and how does it work for HRM?
faqs.answer: Shoolini's PAP track sets the effective fee at Rs 1,50,000, split into Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 per semester) and Rs 30,000 payable only after a confirmed placement is secured. This deferred payment is available from any UGC-DEB approved online MBA in India. HRM graduates who do not secure placement within the agreed window can discuss terms with Shoolini's placement team directly.

faqs.question: Is the Shoolini MBA HRM valid for HR Manager roles at IT companies and banks?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Shoolini MBA is UGC-DEB entitled and NAAC A+ accredited, satisfying management degree requirements at IT companies, banks, and manufacturing firms that specify a recognised university MBA for HR Manager and HRBP roles. The HRM specialisation's curriculum in talent acquisition, performance management, and compensation design is directly tested in HR management role interviews.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover HR analytics and data-driven HR?
faqs.answer: The Statistics for Management subject in Sem 2 builds quantitative foundations for HR analytics including data interpretation and regression basics. The HRM specialisation covers HR analytics applications including attrition modelling and HRIS data interpretation in the spec-specific Sem 3-4 subjects. LinkedIn Learning access provides supplementary courses in HR analytics tools and platforms.

faqs.question: How does Shoolini HRM compare in price to Amity and NMIMS?
faqs.answer: At Rs 1,30,000 (opt-out track), Shoolini is 42% cheaper than Amity (Rs 2,25,000) and 24-28% cheaper than NMIMS (Rs 1,70,000-1,80,000). All three hold NAAC A+ and UGC-DEB approval. Shoolini is the only programme offering Pay After Placement. For candidates outside the Mumbai or metro market where NMIMS and Amity brand premiums are highest, Shoolini offers equivalent credentials at a significant cost advantage.

faqs.question: What labour law content is covered in the HRM specialisation?
faqs.answer: The HRM Sem 3-4 specialisation subjects cover Industrial Disputes Act, Shops and Establishments Act compliance, PF and ESIC statutory requirements, contract labour management, and standing orders. These are directly applicable for HR professionals managing compliance in manufacturing, retail, and service sector organisations. The specifics of Sem 3-4 subject names are confirmed at the admission stage.

chooseIf: You want a Pay After Placement option for your HRM MBA — the only one available from a UGC-DEB approved online university.
Your budget is Rs 1,30,000 or below (opt-out track) with NAAC A+ and NIRF #69.
You are in HR, admin, or operations in North India targeting a formal management credential for manager-level promotion.
You value LinkedIn Learning and KPMG certifications bundled with your degree.
consider: You specifically need Amity's HR analytics specialisation branding for large IT company internal promotion criteria.
You need NMIMS brand recognition specifically for Mumbai-based corporate HR or financial sector people management roles.
You want PwC-co-taught modules and Harvard certificate (consider Chandigarh University MBA HRM).
